response.justification: Candidate has relevant geospatial/GIS domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and strong software craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems via DLang at Sociomantic), but the core requirement — modern commercial C++ or Rust — is barely met. C++ work was a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no Rust experience. Recent roles are predominantly TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, which is a significant mismatch for a C++ routing-tiling pipeline role. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to a strong systems-leaning generalist willing to ramp up on C++.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Open Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background (Sociomantic: millions of requests/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, trunk-based dev, CI/CD, Linux tooling
concerns: No modern commercial C++ experience; last C/C++ role was ~2015 at National Instruments
Recent career trajectory is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, not systems/pipeline engineering
